title: Hornet Removal Long Island City Queens | $350-$550
metaDescription: Professional hornet removal in Long Island City, Queens. Safe nest elimination for homes & businesses near Gantry Plaza. Licensed experts. Call now: $350-$550.
intro: Long Island City residents face unique hornet challenges due to the neighborhood's mix of historic buildings, waterfront parks, and modern high-rises. Our professional hornet removal services protect Queens families and businesses from aggressive hornet nests with safe, effective treatments. We understand the urgency of hornet infestations and provide same-day service throughout Long Island City.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served Long Island City for years with licensed technicians who understand the specific pest pressures of this rapidly growing Queens neighborhood. We offer upfront pricing, guaranteed results, and rapid response times to eliminate hornet threats from your property safely and completely.
localContext: From the waterfront condos near Gantry Plaza State Park to the converted warehouses of the Dutch Kills area, Long Island City's diverse architecture provides numerous opportunities for hornets to build nests in eaves, soffits, and building overhangs. The proximity to green spaces like Murray Playground and the East River waterfront attracts hornets seeking nesting sites near food sources.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove a hornet nest in Long Island City?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day hornet removal service throughout Long Island City and surrounding Queens neighborhoods. Our technicians can typically arrive within 2-4 hours of your call and safely eliminate the nest during the first visit. Emergency weekend service is also available for aggressive hornet situations.

faqs.question: What's included in the $350-$550 hornet removal price?
faqs.answer: Our hornet removal service includes a thorough property inspection, complete nest removal, treatment of the affected area to prevent return activity, and a 30-day warranty. The price varies based on nest location, size, and accessibility. High nests or multiple colonies may require additional equipment and fall toward the higher end of the range.

faqs.question: Are hornets dangerous to Long Island City residents?
faqs.answer: Yes, hornets can be aggressive when defending their nests and their stings are more painful than typical wasps. They can sting multiple times and may attack in groups if threatened. This is especially concerning in Long Island City's densely populated areas where children play in courtyards or residents use rooftop spaces. Professional removal is strongly recommended.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have hornets versus wasps or bees?
faqs.answer: Hornets are larger than typical wasps, measuring up to 1.5 inches long with predominantly black and white or brown and yellow coloring. Their nests are paper-like, often gray or brown, and can be found in trees, under eaves, or in wall voids. If you see large flying insects around a football-sized or larger nest near your Long Island City property, it's likely hornets and requires professional attention.
